fl-001.fp16: A bounded collection of interacting components that together realize a unified purpose within MRL.

fl-002.fp16: The continuous directional movement of data, energy, or intention through MRL channels.

fl-003.fp16: The minimal generative unit from which flows, agents, and structures emerge in MRL.

fl-004.fp16: The immutable central logic that governs particle behavior and law enforcement in MRL.

fl-005.fp16: The outer boundary layer that mediates interaction between MRL internals and external systems.

fl-006.q8: identity mask worn by an agent
fl-006.fp16: A configurable identity profile that determines how an MRL agent presents itself and responds.

fl-007.fp16: The structured storage of past interactions and states that informs future MRL decisions.

fl-008.fp16: A discrete timing event that synchronizes particle flows and agent cycles across MRL.

fl-009.fp16: The reconciliation of state across multiple MRL nodes to maintain coherent system truth.

fl-010.fp16: The contextual environment in which MRL agents operate and exchange particles.

fl-011.fp16: A uniquely addressable processing point within the MRL topology graph.

fl-012.fp16: A named, typed conduit through which particles travel between MRL nodes.

fl-013.fp16: A translation and routing component that connects heterogeneous MRL subsystems.

fl-014.fp16: A logical grouping of MRL nodes that collectively handle a shared workload.

fl-015.fp16: A horizontal abstraction stratum in MRL's hierarchical architecture.

fl-016.fp16: The structural map describing how MRL nodes connect and relate to each other.

fl-017.fp16: A hierarchical branching structure organizing MRL components from root to leaves.

fl-018.fp16: A network topology where every MRL node can directly communicate with peers.

fl-019.fp16: A central MRL node that aggregates and redistributes particles to spoke nodes.

fl-020.fp16: The origin node from which an MRL hierarchy descends and derives authority.

fl-021.fp16: The process of transforming human language or data into MRL particle notation.

fl-022.fp16: The process of converting MRL particle sequences back into human-readable language.

fl-023.fp16: The decision and execution of directing MRL particles along optimal channel paths.

fl-024.fp16: The operation of combining multiple MRL particle streams into a unified coherent flow.

fl-025.fp16: The operation of dividing a single MRL particle stream into multiple parallel flows.

fl-026.fp16: The act of inserting new particles into an already-running MRL flow at a defined point.

fl-027.fp16: The act of retrieving specific particles from an MRL source without altering the source.

fl-028.fp16: The conversion of MRL particles from one format or representation to another.

fl-029.fp16: The process of checking that MRL particles conform to schema and syntax rules.

fl-030.fp16: The reduction of MRL particle stream size while preserving recoverable information.

fl-031.fp16: The elaboration or decompression of condensed MRL particles into fuller representations.

fl-032.fp16: The instantiation of a new MRL agent, thread, or sub-flow from an existing one.

fl-033.fp16: The integration of external particles or knowledge into the MRL internal particle store.

fl-034.fp16: The outward broadcast of MRL particles to one or more receiving channels or systems.

fl-035.fp16: The selection of a qualified subset of particles from a stream based on defined criteria.

fl-036.fp16: The collection and summarization of multiple MRL particle inputs into a single output.

fl-037.fp16: The construction of a fast-lookup map over MRL particles for retrieval by key or hash.

fl-038.fp16: A deterministic fixed-length fingerprint computed from MRL particle content for integrity.

fl-039.fp16: An ed25519 cryptographic signature that proves authorship of an MRL particle or manifest.

fl-040.fp16: The confirmation that an MRL signature was produced by the claimed key holder.

fl-041.fp16: A bounded unit of MRL particle data prepared for transmission across a channel.

fl-042.fp16: A structured descriptor listing all particle references and metadata in an MRL composite.

fl-043.fp16: A fixed-size fragment used to transmit large MRL blobs with resumable capability.

fl-044.fp16: The exchange of capability and identity information to establish a trusted MRL session.

fl-045.fp16: A bounded context window within which MRL agents maintain shared stateful interaction.

fl-046.fp16: A periodic liveness signal sent between MRL nodes to confirm connectivity.

fl-047.fp16: A positive acknowledgment that a transmitted MRL particle was received correctly.

fl-048.fp16: A negative acknowledgment indicating an MRL particle was rejected or corrupted.

fl-049.fp16: The simultaneous emission of an MRL particle to all nodes on a channel.

fl-050.fp16: A directed MRL particle emission from one node to exactly one destination node.

fl-051.fp16: An MRL particle emission directed to a defined subset of nodes in a group.

fl-052.fp16: A named addressable interface through which external systems interact with MRL services.

fl-053.fp16: A shim component that translates between an external protocol and MRL particle format.

fl-054.fp16: A station that receives, optionally transforms, and re-emits MRL particles onward.

fl-055.fp16: The boundary entry point mediating traffic between external systems and MRL internals.

fl-056.fp16: An encapsulated transport path that carries MRL particles through foreign networks.

fl-057.fp16: An ordered buffer holding MRL particles awaiting processing by a downstream consumer.

fl-058.fp16: A temporary holding area that absorbs bursts of MRL particles before forwarding.

fl-059.fp16: A continuous, unbounded sequence of MRL particles flowing through a channel over time.

fl-060.fp16: A bounded group of MRL particles processed atomically as a single unit.

fl-061.fp16: An immutable governing rule within MRL that all particles and agents must obey.

fl-062.fp16: A formal mutual obligation between MRL agents defining expected behaviors and guarantees.

fl-063.fp16: The unique cryptographically provable identifier of an MRL agent or key pair.

fl-064.fp16: The assessed confidence level that determines how much authority an MRL identity is granted.

fl-065.fp16: The delegated power that allows an MRL agent to make decisions on behalf of another.

fl-066.fp16: An immutable labeled snapshot of MRL system or dictionary state at a point in time.

fl-067.fp16: The computed delta describing changes between two successive MRL state versions.

fl-068.fp16: An incremental change set applied to advance an MRL artifact from one version to the next.

fl-069.fp16: The reversion of MRL state to a prior stable version upon detection of failure.

fl-070.fp16: A tamper-evident log recording every action taken on MRL particles for accountability.

fl-071.fp16: The declared purpose behind an MRL agent's action, used for routing and law enforcement.

fl-072.fp16: The surrounding state information that gives meaning to an MRL particle sequence.

fl-073.fp16: The formal structural definition against which MRL particle objects are validated.

fl-074.fp16: A human-readable named pointer anchored to a specific MRL version or particle state.

fl-075.fp16: The provenance record tracing an MRL particle back to its original creator and moment.

fl-076.fp16: The atomic indivisible unit of knowledge or data within the MRL particle system.

fl-077.fp16: The smallest, indivisible element in MRL composition, below the particle abstraction.

fl-078.fp16: A bonded composition of MRL atoms forming a meaningful compound structure.

fl-079.fp16: A propagating pattern of MRL signals that carries modulated information across the field.

fl-080.fp16: The variation of an MRL signal parameter to encode additional information in transmission.

fl-081.fp16: The repetition rate of MRL pulse cycles, used for channel tuning and agent coordination.

fl-082.fp16: The magnitude of an MRL signal, representing importance or priority weight.

fl-083.fp16: Mutual reinforcement between MRL agents that amplifies shared signal patterns.

fl-084.fp16: The extraction and purification of essential particles from a complex source system.

fl-085.fp16: The decomposition of complex structures into discrete MRL particle elements.

fl-086.fp16: The discretization of MRL particle representations at symbol, q4, q8, or fp16 precision.

fl-087.fp16: The persistence of MRL particles to a content-addressed or relational store.

fl-088.fp16: The retrieval of previously stored MRL particles using a key, hash, or query.

fl-089.fp16: A concurrency control mechanism preventing simultaneous mutation of shared MRL state.

fl-090.fp16: The release of an MRL lock, allowing other agents to proceed with mutation.

fl-091.fp16: A point-in-time immutable capture of the full MRL particle store state.

fl-092.fp16: The recovery of MRL particle store state from a previously taken snapshot.

fl-093.fp16: The derivation of new knowledge from existing MRL particles using LLM or rule engines.

fl-094.fp16: A dense vector representation of an MRL particle for similarity search and reasoning.

fl-095.fp16: A structured instruction input passed to an LLM as part of MRL inference pipeline.

fl-096.fp16: The output particles returned by an MRL agent or LLM after processing an input.

fl-097.fp16: A lexical unit produced by splitting text, used as the bridge between human text and particles.

fl-098.fp16: The coherent blending of multiple MRL input sources into a unified particle representation.

fl-099.fp16: A reflected copy of an MRL signal returned to the sender for confirmation or analysis.

fl-100.fp16: The act of switching an MRL agent or flow to a different channel or frequency.
